mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate on equity cash flows and improves liquidity conditions, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than the unconditional sample in periods where FEDFUNDS is falling, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An upward break in UNRATE above recent levels signals labor-market deterioration and weaker household income growth, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the mean forward return after the trigger is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and PAYEMS both accelerate together, the mechanism implies stronger consumer demand and payroll income, supporting revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint highs in RSAFS and PAYEMS do not outperform single-variable signals, or if the interaction term has no positive effect on 20-day forward SPX returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and earnings momentum, which should lift S&P 500 returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: Reject if positive changes in INDPRO fail to predict higher 60-day forward SPX returns than the unconditional distribution, or if the sign of the slope estimate turns negative in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS remains below inflation, real policy rates are falling, financial conditions are easier, and equities should produce positive forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the spread condition does not improve 5-day SPX returns relative to baseline, or if the interaction effect is not positive in at least two-thirds of triggered samples.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in PAYEMS signals weakening payroll creation and softer consumer income growth, which should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: Reject if negative PAYEMS momentum does not precede weaker 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal’s rolling 3-year Rank IC is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST after prior weakness indicates demand stabilization in housing and related cyclicals, which should be bullish for S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if housing-starts breakouts are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the average forward return after the trigger is not above zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a combination of higher FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL compresses valuation multiples and pressures risk assets, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is rising and CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint tightening/inflation condition does not yield lower 20-day SPX returns than either proxy alone, or if the sign of the combined effect becomes positive in rolling out-of-sample tests.
